name: ExtensionServiceRequest_Focus
title: R5: What the service request is about, when it is not about the subject of record (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ServiceRequest.focus` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ServiceRequest.focus` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Resource)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ServiceRequest.focus` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Resource)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ServiceRequest.focus` has a context of ServiceRequest based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ServiceRequest`.
Element `ServiceRequest.focus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: What the service request is about, when it is not about the subject of record (new)
element.comment:
Element `ServiceRequest.focus` has a context of ServiceRequest based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ServiceRequest`.
Element `ServiceRequest.focus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: What the service request is about, when it is not about the subject of record
element.definition: The actual focus of a service request when it is not the subject of record representing something or someone associated with the subject such as a spouse, parent, fetus, or donor. The focus of a service request could also be an existing condition,  an intervention, the subject's diet,  another service request on the subject,  or a body structure such as tumor or implanted device.
